objects.note: DOC-002 §3/§4: reusable, versioned architecture; owns PWU Types; publication flow RPH-DOC-010 §20
DOC-002 §4/§7.1: reusable PWU template owned by a published PWA version
DOC-002 §3: concrete instantiation bound to one PWA version; owns the Professional Work Graph
The durable, event-sourced transcript of the authoring agent's work on a DRAFT PWA — critical domain state (a precursor to the JanumiCode v2 governed stream), not UI metadata.

A durable, event-sourced faithfulness assessment of a DRAFT PWA graph against its authoring prompt, produced by an assessor DISTINCT from the authoring executor (exec != assurance; the assessor's vendor/model is recorded on `assessor`). Authoring-plane sibling to AUTHORING_CONVERSATION — a precursor to the governed stream, NOT the heavyweight execution-plane ASSURANCE_ASSESSMENT.

fields.note: Snapshot of the natural-language prompt the PWA was judged against (the intent).
1-based index within one authoring turn's assess/refine loop.
The previous assessment in the loop (lineage + convergence comparison).
WHO judged — actorType + modelId + providerId carry the separation-of-duties record (a different vendor than the executor).
Integer 0..100 (percent). The canonical content-hash forbids non-integer numbers, so scores persist as integer percents; the assessor port uses 0..1 and converts at the persistence boundary.
Prompt-derived judging dimensions + per-criterion score (GENERIC, not SDLC-hardcoded).
Specific gaps the judge found; drive the auto-refinement directive.
overallScore delta vs priorAssessment (convergence signal), integer percent points; set when iteration >= 2.
Convergence signal vs prior (the stuck-loop stop mechanism); set when iteration >= 2.
Lifecycle disposition (distinct from the judge's verdict). RECORDED=judged; ESCALATED=awaiting human; RESOLVED=human decided.
Escalation reason (set on ESCALATED).
Human-readable escalation context to help the human decide (set on ESCALATED).
The human's decision (set on RESOLVED).
The human (or actor) who resolved the escalation (set on RESOLVED).

conflicts.resolution: Both DOC-002 extractions claimed the envelope has NO schemaVersion and an EXTRA optional authorityId?. The ratified codebase (packages/rph-contracts/src/envelopes.ts objectEnvelopeShape) is authoritative and RESOLVES this: schemaVersion IS present; authorityId is DROPPED (explicit comment 'authorityId (DOC-002 §4) is DROPPED per docs §5'). The task-prompt envelope description (schemaVersion in, no authorityId) is correct. schemaVersion is one of the version quartet (contract/schema/semantic/revision).
DOC-002 §6.1 = required; DOC-007 §10.1 = optional (formalizedObjective?). DOC-007 (latest serialized) wins → optional.
DOC-002 §7.1 = 'boundary'; DOC-007 §11.1 = 'boundaries' (same WorkBoundary shape). DOC-007 wins → 'boundaries'.

decompositionContractId?, recompositionContractId?, currentBaselineId? appear in DOC-002 §7.1 but are ABSENT from DOC-007 §11.1. Per 'include every field from both' they are retained as OPTIONAL, flagged as open (DOC-007 may deliberately drop them).

conflicts.topic: ASSURANCE_POLICY envelope vs standalone + reshaped fields
conflicts.resolution: DOC-002 §17.1 models ASSURANCE_POLICY as extending ObjectEnvelope with fields targetTypes/applicabilityRule/requiredEvidenceRequirements/validatorRole/failureSeverity/remediationPolicy/escalationPolicy/waiverPolicy?. DOC-007 §17 defines a STANDALONE AssurancePolicyDefinition (own id/version/semanticVersion, no envelope) with a completely reshaped field set (applicableObjectTypes, applicability:ApplicabilityExpression, defaultClaimTemplates, requiredEvidence+optionalEvidence, criteria:AssessmentCriterion[], evaluatorRole, findingDefinitions, dispositionRules, permittedControlActions, remediationRules/escalationRules/waiverRules). policy-level failureSeverity is DROPPED (moves to FindingDefinition.defaultSeverity). DOC-007 wins. Note tension: ProfessionalWorkObjectTypeSchema still lists ASSURANCE_POLICY as an envelope objectType — see open items.

DOC-002 §18 field 'disposition' (AssuranceDispositionSchema, 6 terminal values) is REPLACED on the object by DOC-007 §19 'assessmentState' (AssuranceAssessmentStateSchema, 15-value state machine incl. VALIDATOR_FAILED/INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION). The 5-value terminal disposition (AssuranceDispositionRecommendationSchema) now lives on the AssuranceAssessmentCompleted event (§19.3), not the object. DOC-007 wins.

evaluator: DOC-002 required → DOC-007 optional (wins). recommendedControlAction? (single optional inline enum, DOC-002) → recommendedControlActions (required array of ControlActionRecommendation, DOC-007) (wins). startedAt/completedAt/confidence are DOC-002-only, retained-flagged.

DOC-002 §20.1 references by id array (stepIds: string[], transitionIds: string[]); DOC-007 §15 EMBEDS full object arrays (steps: ExecutionStep[], transitions: ExecutionTransition[]). DOC-007 wins → embedded.
DOC-002 §24.1 opaque itemObjectIds: string[]; DOC-007 §23 version-pinned itemObjectVersions: BaselineItemVersion[] ({objectId, semanticVersion, contentHash?}) + new purpose/scope. DOC-007 wins.

DOC-002 §17.1 listed 5 values (NONE|DIFFERENT_INVOCATION|DIFFERENT_AGENT|DIFFERENT_MODEL|HUMAN). Codebase IndependenceRequirementSchema (DOC-004 §8.1) is the authoritative 8-value superset (adds DIFFERENT_CONTEXT_INSTANCE, DIFFERENT_PROVIDER, ORGANIZATIONALLY_INDEPENDENT). Use the codebase schema.

conflicts.topic: Enums flagged 'not in provided list' actually exist
conflicts.resolution: The extractions flagged ActorType, AuthorityType, AssurancePolicyStatus, CriterionResult.result, and ValidatorResult.dispositionRecommendation as having no closed-enum schema. All DO exist in codebase enums.ts: ActorTypeSchema, AuthorityTypeSchema, AssurancePolicyStatusSchema, CriterionResultOutcomeSchema, and AssuranceDispositionRecommendationSchema (5-value, distinct from the 6-value AssuranceDispositionSchema). Wired accordingly.

Not a merge — two distinct closed enums. AssuranceSeveritySchema = 5 values (adds INFORMATIONAL); FailureSeveritySchema = 4 values. AssuranceObservation.severity → AssuranceSeveritySchema. (failureSeverity no longer appears at policy level in DOC-007.)

No conflict but load-bearing: Decision.authority uses ActorReference (both DOC-002 §23.1 and DOC-007 §22), NOT AuthorityReference. Only Obligation.authority and Constraint.authority use AuthorityReference.

openItems.item: ARTIFACT object has no field definition anywhere
openItems.why: ARTIFACT is one of the 17 ProfessionalWorkObjectType values but neither DOC-002 nor DOC-007 defines an ArtifactObject interface/schema (only the ArtifactReference helper and ARTIFACT EvidenceType value exist). Fields are to-be-designed; do not fabricate.

openItems.item: 6 objects lack a DOC-007 serialized field contract
openItems.why: OBLIGATION, CONSTRAINT, DECOMPOSITION_CONTRACT, RECOMPOSITION_CONTRACT, RUNTIME_BINDING (and ARTIFACT) have no field-level definition in the latest serialized contract (DOC-007). CONSTRAINT + RUNTIME_BINDING have schema FILES listed in DOC-007 §3 but no prose; the others have neither. All are sourced from DOC-002 prose and need serialization before codegen. Their enums (ObligationType/Strength/Status, ConstraintType/Strength/Status, CoverageType, DecompositionContractStatus, RecompositionContractStatus, AuthorizationStatus, StepType/State) exist but have no wired consumer field yet.

openItems.item: ~40 helper sub-types are referenced but not field-defined
openItems.why: Only 12 helpers are field-defined (ActorReference, AuthorityReference, ProvenanceRecord, WorkRiskProfile, WorkBoundary, CoverageClaim, ExecutionStep, ApplicabilityExpression, BaselineItemVersion, ValidatorResult, CriterionResult, TraceLink). The rest (DesiredOutcome, SuccessCondition, ArtifactRequirement, OutputDefinition, ArtifactReference, ApplicabilityRule, decomposition/recomposition rule+propagation+allocation types, execution-plan policy types, runtime-binding policy/capability types, execution-step bindings/conditions, and all AssurancePolicyDefinition helper arrays) need field definitions. Several have JSON-schema files listed in DOC-007 §3 (evidence-requirement, disposition-rule, control-action-recommendation, artifact-reference) that should be read for their shapes.

DOC-002 Constraint.applicability uses helper ApplicabilityRule; DOC-007 AssurancePolicyDefinition.applicability uses the field-defined ApplicabilityExpression (§18 op union). Ratify sheet C plans to unify them into one DSL (M7). Decide whether Constraint.applicability adopts ApplicabilityExpression.

openItems.item: ASSURANCE_POLICY: envelope PWO vs standalone definition
openItems.why: ProfessionalWorkObjectTypeSchema still enumerates ASSURANCE_POLICY as an envelope-extending objectType, but DOC-007 §17 models AssurancePolicyDefinition as standalone (own id/version/semanticVersion, no envelope). Confirm whether ASSURANCE_POLICY is a full PWO or a standalone definition object, and reconcile the objectType enum accordingly.

Outside the three extractions but load-bearing: the ratify sheet (A-3) retained an optional riskProfiles field on AssurancePolicyDefinition — required by DOC-004 §3.1, absent from DOC-007 §17 serialized (suspected unintended omission). Confirm it belongs, then add it as optional.

PWU.decompositionContractId?/recompositionContractId?/currentBaselineId? and AssuranceAssessment.startedAt/completedAt?/confidence? are present in DOC-002 but absent from the DOC-007 serialized contract. Retained (optional) per union rule but flagged — confirm keep vs drop for the serialized shape (startedAt/completedAt likely superseded by event-envelope occurredAt/recordedAt).

openItems.item: TraceLink and ExecutionStep are id-bearing but not PWO envelope objects
openItems.why: Both are field-defined and carry their own id (idPrefix 'trace' / 'step') but do NOT extend ObjectEnvelope and are NOT among the 17 ProfessionalWorkObjectType. Modeled here in the helper-sub-type catalog (not the 17-object list). ExecutionTransition and ExecutionStep also have persistence tables in §31.

openItems.item: ControlAction is an enum, not a helper object
openItems.why: DOC-007's helper listing tagged ControlAction as a referenced helper; it is actually the 23-value ControlActionSchema enum. AssurancePolicyDefinition.permittedControlActions is ControlAction[] with enumRef ControlActionSchema — excluded from the helper catalog. ControlActionRecommendation (whose .action ∈ ControlActionSchema) IS a distinct helper object and is retained.
